meta.note: Bay Area seed compiled from public sources. Every record is flagged needs_verification: addresses, hours, and phone numbers MUST be confirmed with each provider before anyone relies on them. Coordinates are approximate.

services.name: St. Anthony's Dining Room
services.description: Free hot meals served daily, no questions asked.
services.address: 121 Golden Gate Ave, San Francisco, CA 94102

services.name: GLIDE Daily Free Meals
services.description: Three free meals a day, 364 days a year. No ID, no cost, no questions.

services.name: LavaMaeˣ Mobile Showers
services.description: Free mobile showers and hygiene services. Schedule and locations vary by day.

services.name: Tenderloin Technology Lab
services.description: Free computer access and digital literacy education.
services.address: 121 Golden Gate Ave, San Francisco, CA 94102

services.name: Hospitality House Shelter
services.description: Free emergency shelter and drop-in services for adults experiencing homelessness.

services.name: Homeless Advocacy Project
services.description: Free legal advice, advocacy, and services information for people who are homeless or low-income.

services.name: HealthRIGHT 360 Clinic
services.description: Free and low-cost integrated medical, dental, and behavioral health care. Sliding scale.

services.name: St. Vincent de Paul Free Dining Room
services.description: Free hot dining room for people who are homeless or low-income.

services.name: Alameda County Community Food Bank
services.description: Regional food bank; call the helpline to be connected to a nearby pantry or hot meal the same day.

services.name: CityTeam Oakland
services.description: Hot breakfast and dinner, clothing, and shelter services.

services.name: Dorothy Day House Drop-in Center
services.description: Daily drop-in: meals, showers, laundry, clothing, lockers, mail, internet, housing support. Open 365 days.

services.name: Women's Daytime Drop-in Center
services.description: Daytime drop-in and housing resources for women and families; food and hygiene services.

services.name: Berkeley Food & Housing Project
services.description: Emergency shelter, meals, and housing services. Call the shelter hotline for reservations.

services.name: Sacred Heart Community Service
services.description: Food pantry, clothes closet, and housing/benefits assistance. Must reside in Santa Clara County.

services.name: CityTeam San Jose
services.description: Daily hot meals, mobile food pantries, showers, clothing, and transitional housing.
services.address: 1174 Old Bayshore Hwy, San Jose, CA 95112
services.hours: Hot meals daily, call to confirm

services.name: LifeMoves Georgia Travis Center
services.description: Shelter and services for women and children experiencing homelessness.

services.name: Samaritan House
services.description: Free medical and dental clinics, food, shelter, and case management for San Mateo County.

services.name: LifeMoves Maple Street Shelter
services.description: Emergency shelter and supportive services for families and adults.

services.name: St. Vincent de Paul Free Dining Room (Marin)
services.description: Marin's primary free meals program. Open every day of the year; 500+ meals daily.

services.name: St. Vincent de Paul Housing Help Desk
services.description: Housing assessment, temporary shelter placement, and homelessness prevention. Located above the Free Dining Room.

services.name: Ritter Center
services.description: Marin's primary healthcare provider for people experiencing homelessness: medical, behavioral health, showers, food pantry.
services.verification_note: Ritter announced a move to 800 A St from 16 Ritter St -- confirm current location.

services.name: Homeward Bound of Marin - New Beginnings Center
services.description: Emergency shelter and services for adults, with job training via Fresh Starts Culinary Academy. In Novato.

services.name: Homeward Bound of Marin - Family Center
services.description: Emergency and transitional housing for families with children in Marin.

services.name: SF Rapid Response Hotline
services.organization: San Francisco Immigrant Legal & Education Network (SFILEN)
services.description: 24-hour hotline to report ICE enforcement, verify activity, and activate legal support. English, Spanish, Cantonese.
services.verification_note: Hotline number; not a walk-in address.

services.name: Marin Rapid Response Network
services.description: Marin County hotline to report and respond to immigration enforcement activity.
services.verification_note: Hotline number; countywide.

services.name: ACILEP Hotline (Alameda County)
services.organization: Alameda County Immigration Legal & Education Partnership
services.description: Rapid-response hotline for urgent ICE activity or detentions; mobilizes legal support and advocacy in Alameda County.
services.verification_note: Hotline number; countywide.

services.name: Centro Legal de la Raza
services.description: Free immigration legal services, removal/deportation defense, and know-your-rights presentations. Serving Oakland since 1969.

services.name: La Raza Centro Legal
services.description: Free immigration removal-defense legal services for Bay Area residents in removal proceedings; consultations for detained and non-detained people.

services.name: Bay Area Legal Aid
services.description: One of the largest legal-aid providers in the Bay Area (seven counties); immigration practice focused on humanitarian and vulnerable cases.

services.name: Jazzie's Place LGBT Shelter
services.description: 24-bed emergency shelter serving LGBT homeless adults, with sectioned areas for male, female, and gender-nonconforming guests. Part of the Dolores Street shelter.

services.name: SF LGBT Center Access Point
services.description: Coordinated-entry access point for LGBTQ youth (18-27) experiencing or facing homelessness: housing assessments, shelter connection, case management, drop-in.

services.name: Oakland LGBTQ Community Center
services.description: Resource hub connecting LGBTQ youth, adults, and seniors to emergency, shared, and permanent housing, plus health referrals and community services.

services.name: LGBTQ Youth Space
services.description: Drop-in center for LGBTQ+ youth and young adults 13-25 in Santa Clara County: peer counseling, support groups, case management, safer-sex supplies, psychiatry.

services.name: New Haven Inn (LGBTQ+ inclusive shelter)
services.description: Inclusive emergency shelter in downtown San Jose with focused support for people who identify as LGBTQ+. Location kept confidential for client safety; call or text to connect.
services.verification_note: Shelter location is confidential for safety; contact by phone/text.

services.name: SF Food Not Bombs (Wednesday)
services.description: Free hot vegetarian/vegan meals shared in public, no questions asked. Wednesday sharing at 16th & Mission BART Plaza.
services.address: 16th & Mission BART Plaza, San Francisco, CA 94103
services.verification_note: Volunteer-run; schedule/location shift. Confirm on sffnb.org before going.

services.name: SF Food Not Bombs (Friday)
services.description: Free hot vegetarian/vegan meals. Friday sharing near the SF Public Library at Larkin & Grove, across from City Hall.
services.address: Larkin St & Grove St, San Francisco, CA 94102
services.verification_note: Volunteer-run; schedule/location shift. Confirm on sffnb.org before going.

services.name: East Bay Food Not Bombs
services.description: Free hot vegan/vegetarian meals shared at People's Park, Berkeley, five days a week, plus rotating Oakland locations. Serving since 1991.
services.address: People's Park, 2556 Haste St, Berkeley, CA 94704
services.verification_note: Volunteer-run; Oakland days/locations rotate. Confirm on eastbayfoodnotbombs.org.

services.name: SF AIDS Foundation 6th Street Center
services.description: Syringe access & disposal, overdose prevention (naloxone, fentanyl test strips), hep C and HIV testing, substance-use counseling. Fixed site in Tenderloin/SoMa.

services.name: Santa Clara County Harm Reduction Program
services.organization: County of Santa Clara Public Health
services.description: Needs-based syringe distribution and disposal, sharps containers, naloxone, and rapid HIV/hep C/syphilis testing. No restrictions.
services.hours: See schedule; arrive 30 min before close for testing
services.verification_note: Multiple mobile sites; confirm current schedule with the program.

services.name: HEPPAC Syringe Access
services.organization: HIV Education & Prevention Project of Alameda County
services.description: Syringe exchange, naloxone (OPEND), HIV/hep C testing, and a drop-in center (showers, laundry, food). Fixed sites + mobile across Oakland and Contra Costa.
services.address: Fruitvale, E 12th St & 23rd Ave, Oakland, CA 94606
services.hours: Tue 6-8pm Fruitvale; call for other sites
services.verification_note: Multiple fixed + mobile sites with different hours; confirm current schedule.

services.name: Berkeley NEED
services.description: Volunteer-run needle exchange and naloxone distribution. Look for the black van near the Berkeley Free Clinic; anonymous, no dirty-needle requirement.
services.verification_note: Van-based, rotating weekly sites; confirm days/times on berkeleyneed.org.

services.name: Punks With Lunch Oakland
services.description: Volunteer mutual-aid group providing harm-reduction supplies, naloxone, and sack lunches via outreach in West Oakland and beyond.
services.verification_note: Street outreach; no fixed address. Confirm schedule on their site/Instagram.

services.name: Spiral Gardens
services.organization: Spiral Gardens Community Food Security Project
services.description: Community farm where volunteers grow organic food together; the harvest is shared among volunteers and given free to people who need it. Plant nursery on site.

services.name: Alemany Farm
services.description: San Francisco's largest urban farm (3.5 acres). Free produce and donation-based workshops; no one turned away for lack of funds. Volunteer workdays open to all.

services.name: Homies Empowerment Freedom Farm
services.description: Community-led East Oakland farm that gives away free food and teaches people how to grow it cheaply. 28 open garden boxes; soil, seeds, and water provided.

services.name: Long Haul Infoshop
services.description: All-volunteer anarchist resource center: radical lending library, zine-making space, free internet, reading room, and meeting space. Free literature.

services.name: Omni Commons
services.description: Collectively-run community commons housing nine collectives — hackerspace, biolab, free spaces, and event halls — run on consensus and radical inclusion.

services.name: Mandela Grocery Cooperative
services.description: Worker-owned cooperative grocery in West Oakland focused on affordable, healthy food access in a historically underserved neighborhood.

services.name: Mandela Partners CSA
services.description: Weekly produce box from local BIPOC farms. Sliding-scale pricing and a 50% discount for SNAP/CalFresh (Fresh Creds). Home delivery or pickup in Alameda County.

services.name: Acta Non Verba Youth CSA
services.description: Youth-run urban-farm CSA in East Oakland; pay-what-you-can options, BIPOC farmer network. Pickup in Oakland/Berkeley or delivery across the East Bay.
services.verification_note: Seasonal CSA; confirm current signup window and pickup sites.

services.name: Sonoma County farm CSA (South Berkeley pickup)
services.description: Sonoma County family-farm CSA (vegetables, fruit, herbs, eggs), May-November, with a South Berkeley pickup site. Supports a small local farm directly.
services.address: South Berkeley Farmers Market, Adeline St & 63rd St, Berkeley, CA 94703
services.verification_note: Sonoma-based; pickup site and season vary by farm. Confirm before subscribing.

services.name: Berkeley Free Clinic
services.description: Volunteer-run DIY health collective since 1969. Free medical care, TB/STI/HIV testing, peer counseling, and resource navigation. No fees, ever. Also runs a dental lottery.

services.name: St. Anthony Free Medical Clinic
services.description: Free primary and urgent care for homeless, low-income, and uninsured people. Drop-in triage; all are welcome regardless of ability to pay.
services.address: 150 Golden Gate Ave, San Francisco, CA 94102

services.name: Native American Health Center
services.description: Community clinic offering medical, dental, behavioral health, and WIC on a sliding fee scale; Medi-Cal accepted. Open to all, all backgrounds welcome.

services.name: Berkeley Free Clinic Dental
services.description: Free lifetime dental care to a limited number of people via monthly lottery, prioritizing uninsured/unhoused/low-income; separate Friday service for immigrants & refugees without Denti-Cal.
services.hours: Dental lottery — currently paused; call to confirm
services.verification_note: Dental services paused during a space build-out; confirm status before relying.

services.name: Native American Health Center Dental
services.description: Sliding-scale and Medi-Cal/Denti-Cal dental care; part of a full-service community clinic. Open to all.

services.name: SF Essential Trip Card & Free Muni
services.description: Free Muni for low-income seniors (65+) and people with disabilities, plus the Essential Trip Card (pay 20% of taxi fare) for essential trips. Apply via 311.
services.verification_note: Program, not a location; eligibility applies. Call 311 or 415-701-2311.

services.name: Berkeley Rides for Seniors & Disabled
services.description: Free taxi, wheelchair van, and Uber/Lyft (via GoGo) rides for Berkeley seniors 70+ and disabled residents 18+. Extra vouchers for medical trips.
services.hours: Apply by mail/email; waitlist may apply
services.verification_note: Waitlist may be at capacity; confirm current status with the program.

services.name: Medi-Cal Free Medical Transportation
services.description: If you have Medi-Cal and no other way to get to a health appointment, your plan covers free rides (including wheelchair/litter van). Arrange via your plan ~7 days ahead.
services.hours: Call your Medi-Cal plan 7+ days before appointment
services.verification_note: Benefit, not a location; contact your specific Medi-Cal plan to arrange.

services.name: La Casa de las Madres
services.description: 24-hour domestic-violence crisis line, confidential emergency shelter, counseling, and safety planning. Adult line 877-503-1850; teen line 877-923-0700; text 415-200-3575.
services.address: San Francisco, CA (24-hour hotline; shelter location confidential)
services.verification_note: Shelter location is confidential for safety. Call the hotline; do not seek a walk-in address.

services.name: W.O.M.A.N. Inc.
services.organization: Women Organized to Make Abuse Non-Existent
services.description: 24-hour domestic-violence support line, peer counseling, and referrals; includes services for lesbian and bisexual women. Confidential.
services.verification_note: Support line; not a walk-in shelter address.

services.name: Family Violence Law Center
services.description: Alameda County crisis line plus free legal advocacy, restraining-order help, and counseling for survivors of domestic and dating violence.

services.name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
services.description: 24/7 confidential national hotline for anyone affected by domestic violence. Call, text START to 88788, or chat online. Spanish and interpretation available.
services.verification_note: National hotline, not a location.

services.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
services.description: Free, confidential 24/7 support for suicidal, mental-health, or substance-use crisis. Call or text 988, or chat online. For anyone in distress or worried about someone.
services.verification_note: National line, not a location.

services.name: California Peer-Run Warm Line
services.organization: Mental Health Association of San Francisco
services.description: Free, non-emergency emotional support by peers with lived experience — for anyone in California, before a moment becomes a crisis. Phone, text, or chat, 24/7, multiple languages.
services.verification_note: Statewide warm line, not a location; non-emergency support.

services.name: Crisis Support Services of Alameda County
services.organization: Crisis Support Services of Alameda County
services.description: 24-hour crisis and suicide-prevention counseling (call 988 or 800-309-2131), grief support, and community programs. Translation in 140+ languages.
services.verification_note: Crisis line; the office is not a walk-in service.

services.name: MH First Oakland
services.description: Community, non-police response to mental-health crises — psychiatric emergencies, substance-use support, and DV situations — Fri/Sat nights. A volunteer-run alternative to calling 911.
services.verification_note: Mobile, weekend-night hours; confirm current operating hours with APTP.

services.name: SF Women Against Rape (SFWAR)
services.description: 24-hour rape-crisis line offering support, information, and advocacy for survivors of rape and sexual assault, their friends and family. Serves all survivors.
services.verification_note: Crisis line; confidential. Not a walk-in address.

services.name: Alameda Health SARRT (Highland Hospital)
services.organization: Alameda Health System — Sexual Assault Response & Recovery Team
services.description: Sexual-assault and domestic-violence medical response and recovery services based at Highland Hospital in Oakland: forensic exams, advocacy, and follow-up care. (Now the primary Alameda County referral after BAWAR closed in 2025.)
services.verification_note: Replaced BAWAR, which closed permanently in Sept 2025. Confirm current intake/response process.

services.name: National Sexual Assault Hotline (RAINN)
services.description: Free, confidential 24/7 national hotline connecting survivors of sexual assault to local support. Call 800-656-4673 or chat online.
services.verification_note: National hotline, not a location.

services.name: CROP — Ready 4 Life
services.description: Reentry program designed by formerly incarcerated leaders: 12-month career development, case management, stipends, and supportive housing in Oakland. Rolling applications.
services.hours: Apply on rolling basis; see website
services.verification_note: Application-based residential program; contact for eligibility and openings.

services.name: Reentry SF
services.description: Case management and reentry planning for people leaving incarceration: employment services, transitional-housing help, peer support, and support groups.
services.verification_note: Program with intake process, not a walk-in location.

services.name: Transitions Clinic Network
services.description: Primary medical care and social-work support designed for and staffed by formerly incarcerated people; trains and hires community health workers with lived experience.

services.name: AbortionFinder (verified clinic search)
services.description: The largest directory of verified, trusted abortion providers in the US, updated weekly. Search by location and gestational age. Screens out fake 'crisis pregnancy centers.'
services.verification_note: Use this to find a real provider; it excludes fake clinics (CPCs). Not a location.

services.name: ACCESS Reproductive Justice
services.description: California's abortion fund and practical-support network: free confidential info and referrals, help with the cost of care, plus travel, lodging, childcare, and 100+ trained volunteers offering rides and doula support. Mutual-aid model.
services.verification_note: Statewide fund/support network, not a walk-in location. Healthline 1-800-376-4636.

services.name: National Abortion Federation Hotline
services.description: Toll-free hotline connecting callers to verified abortion providers and financial assistance nationwide. Confidential.
services.verification_note: National hotline (1-800-772-9100), not a location.

services.name: Planned Parenthood (find a health center)
services.description: 100+ California health centers providing abortion and full reproductive/sexual health care; most patients use Medi-Cal. Use the site or phone line to find and book a verified center near you.
services.verification_note: Use the finder for a verified center; individual locations vary. Not a single address.

services.name: Repro Legal Helpline
services.description: Free, confidential legal help about abortion rights — including minors' options and self-managed abortion. For anyone unsure of their legal situation.
services.verification_note: Legal helpline (1-844-868-2812), not a location.

services.name: Pit Stop — UN Plaza
services.organization: San Francisco Public Works — Pit Stop
services.description: Staffed, clean public toilet with running water, soap, and a used-needle receptacle. Open 24 hours. Part of SF's Pit Stop program.
services.address: UN Plaza, Market & 7th St, San Francisco, CA 94102
services.verification_note: Staffed public toilet; hours can change. Report issues via 311.

services.name: Pit Stop — 16th & Mission
services.organization: San Francisco Public Works — Pit Stop
services.description: Staffed 24-hour public toilet at the 16th St BART plaza. Running water, soap, needle receptacle.
services.verification_note: Staffed public toilet; hours can change. Report issues via 311.

services.name: Pit Stop — Civic Center
services.organization: San Francisco Public Works — Pit Stop
services.description: Staffed public toilet at Grove & Larkin near Civic Center. Part of SF's Pit Stop program.
services.address: Grove & Larkin St, San Francisco, CA 94102
services.verification_note: Staffed public toilet; hours vary by site. Confirm via sfpublicworks.org/pitstop.

services.name: Pit Stop — 24th & Mission
services.organization: San Francisco Public Works — Pit Stop
services.description: Staffed public toilet at 24th & Mission. Running water, soap, needle receptacle.
services.verification_note: Staffed public toilet; hours can change. Report issues via 311.

services.name: SF Public Bathroom Map (open data)
services.description: City-maintained open dataset of public restrooms and drinking fountains across San Francisco, combining Rec & Parks, PUC, and Public Works assets.
services.verification_note: A dataset of many restrooms, not one location; individual hours/access vary.
